title: red orange cyan
wikitext:
[]: red - orange - cyan - ???             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed Mar 06, 2013 2:31 pm SOME thought must have gone into this... why did they choose those colors and what could they mean?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 06, 2013 3:13 pm And Final's "color" is white.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Wed Mar 06, 2013 3:25 pm Isn't the color white in Japan linked to death?

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed Mar 06, 2013 3:33 pm The colour ao (青) is a confusing one, since not only are blue skies said to be aozora (青空), but green traffic lights are also ao. According to Gendai Kokugo Reikai Jiten published by Shogakukan, ao is the Japanese name for the printing ink colour "cyan", and it is used for both green and for sky blue and sea blue. The Koujien dictionary's entry also says that ao is the name of sky blue, sea blue and green. It also explains that according to one historical explanation, in ancient Japan the only colours were aka, shiro, kuro and ao. This dictionary also notes that historically aoi meant a medatanu iro, a "dull colour", and was used even for grey. It now covers colours as far apart as ai, a dark blue, midori, green, and sou, the colour of grass.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Wed Mar 06, 2013 5:35 pm qu4d wrote: Japan, why are you so complicated

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Mar 06, 2013 5:45 pm It's me, or every new cover have the lines of the words thicker than the precedent?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Mar 06, 2013 7:08 pm ElMariachi wrote: It's me, or every new cover have the lines of the words thicker than the precedent?

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Mar 06, 2013 7:46 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: As to the color white, it does indeed symbolize death. So does the number 4, or 四. The number four is pronounced "Shi", which is similar to the character for death, which is 死, or "Si". Also, FINAL is going to be the fourth movie. As far as I can tell, there's a helluva lot of symbolism surrounding this.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Mar 06, 2013 7:48 pm CJD wrote: May god have mercy on us, for Anno shall not.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Mar 06, 2013 8:06 pm Oh yes he can. We'll know when FINAL premieres in Japan simply because of the frightened screams of emotional release and serenity that is usually thought to accompany a real life Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Mar 06, 2013 8:08 pm Bad end seems too obvious now. Hope we get a good one.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 07, 2013 12:06 am Are we 100% certain Final's color is white? That could just be a placeholder color, lots of sites have white as backgrounds. But they could be going with white, of course. Anywho, considering all the rainbows in New Movie Edition, having the color spectrum sort of going on for the 4 films makes sense to me.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Thu Mar 07, 2013 12:40 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Are we 100% certain Final's color is white? That could just be a placeholder color,

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Mar 07, 2013 2:58 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Well, he can't kill everybody.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Mar 07, 2013 4:59 am pwhodges wrote: It happened in Texhnolyze ...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Mar 07, 2013 7:18 am All of the backgrounds for the PREVIEW screens have been white. I could also be that the placeholder page for the FINAL movie on the official website is simply keeping in spirit of the PREVIEW screens.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Mar 07, 2013 8:52 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: All of the backgrounds for the PREVIEW screens have been white. I could also be that the placeholder page for the FINAL movie on the official website is simply keeping in spirit of the PREVIEW screens.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Thu Mar 07, 2013 3:07 pm Surely if they're calling it "FINAL" rather than "4.0", they would be trying to avert the east Asian fear of the number "4"? Ah, who am I kidding. FINAL looks like it could be another EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Mar 07, 2013 6:53 pm Final's colour is going to be white: http://www.evangelion.co.jp/

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Mar 07, 2013 9:13 pm driftking18594 wrote: Surely if they're calling it "FINAL" rather than "4.0", they would be trying to avert the east Asian fear of the number "4"? Ah, who am I kidding. FINAL looks like it could be another EoE.
